subject: Re: LSJ Projects - Woodshop gate report
body:
the question was johns work, since john told me that three weeks ago he had gottne hardware for the gate.. john did not restain anything at tiki, he did not use pebble. he is a carpenter.. s

----- Original Message ----
From: "Miles Alexander, LSJ" <littlestjames@yahoo.com>
To: Jeffery E Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: Darren Indyke <dkiesq@aol.com>; Emad Hanna <ehanna@nysgmail.com>
Sent: Friday, December 15, 2006 2:36:41 PM
Subject: LSJ Projects - Woodshop gate report

Greetings
   
  Regarding the status of the gate.
   
   John Wertz and his team consist of : John as lead carpenter, cement and layout person, David is his assistant and also a painter. Gerry is a painter, Francis and his assistant Jeff are responsible for cement work, masonry and tiling. I assist as needed.
   
  JE requested a list of projects that have resulted in the project not starting. Bearing in mind that this project was always stated as been last on the priority list, the following has happened. (Not necessary in chronological order)
   
  1.Install Black Fin windscreen
  2.Woodshop extension - prep area, cast the footings, erect the shed, build the trellis to hide the shed from road, construct the deck for storing wood.(Started 9/15/05 finished 11/3 per report date)
  3. Shaded area design and send to JE
  4. Blue and yellow
 buildings - repair screens and floors
  5. Dining table chairs - install wheels
  6. Great Room Speaker covers
  7. MBR trellis install and partial dismantle
  8. Gym cement polish, prep area for contractor to start
  9. Dock underwater lights - lift and then replace wooden planks as required.
  10. Flag pole Olive grove - build plant surrounds
  11. Dock - install pebble surround
  12. Main road entrance - install pebble threshold
  13. Tiki house roof - replace the thatch
  14. Tiki- Build extension of landing from kitchen to main deck
  15. Tiki Restain the entire area
  16. African Archer - Unpack and then prepare for moving and final placement
  17. Dock - Repair dock light pedestals
  18. Woodshop - Plan and repair leaks in the side walls 
  19. Pool area - Plan and prepare for shade cloth covering over seating area. We
 planned and bought the wood. Project canceled by JE
  20. Parking area - Build sugar mill windows, stressed and stained.
  21. Dock - Haul out and repair broken parts of the floating dock.
  22. Road drainage -Install steel grates over road
  23. Flagpole - Install new flagpole overlooking Dog Island
  24. Helipad - Finalize design, prepare and cast new landing at heli pad, tile and grout. 
  25. Pool area - Remove light poles and relocate at  a/c unit.
  26. Pool area - close gap between walls
  Pool area - Repair the existing tiles
  27. Pool area - Paint in pattern approved by JE (Ongoing)
  28. Dock carpet - Install new carpet on dock
  29. General maintenance - For example: Main kitchen cupboard doors, sink repairs shutters broken, Main office floor lifting
  30. Staff cottage - Repair the a/c ducting that hides the pipes.
  Laundry -
 paint the a/c unit newly installed.
   
  Trust that this helps
   
  Miles
   
   
   


    Mr Miles Alexander 
  Little St James Island, 6100 Red Hook Qrts B3, St Thomas, USVI, USA. 00802-1348
  Phone : 340-5139855, Fax : 340-7758108, Email : littlestjames@yahoo.com
   







__________________________________________________

http://mail.yahoo.com

Sent: Friday, December 15, 2006 2:36:41 PM
Subject: LSJ Projects - Woodshop gate report

Greetings
 
Regarding the status of the gate.
 
 John Wertz and his team consist of : John as lead carpenter, cement and layout person, David is his assistant and also a painter. Gerry is a painter, Francis and his assistant Jeff are responsible for cement work, masonry and tiling. I assist as needed.
 
JE requested a list of projects that have resulted in the project not starting. Bearing in mind that this project was always stated as been last on the priority list, the following has happened. (Not necessary in chronological order)
 
1.Install Black Fin windscreen
2.Woodshop extension - prep area, cast the footings, erect the shed, build the trellis to hide the shed from road, construct the deck for storing wood.(Started 9/15/05 finished 11/3 per report date)
3. Shaded area design and send to JE
4. Blue and yellow buildings - repair screens and floors
5. Dining table chairs - install wheels
6. Great Room Speaker covers
7. MBR trellis install and partial dismantle
8. Gym cement polish, prep area for contractor to start
9. Dock underwater lights - lift and then replace wooden planks as required.
10. Flag pole Olive grove - build plant surrounds
11. Dock - install pebble surround
12. Main road entrance - install pebble threshold
13. Tiki house roof - replace the thatch
14. Tiki- Build extension of landing from kitchen to main deck
15. Tiki Restain the entire area
16. African Archer - Unpack and then prepare for moving and final placement
17. Dock - Repair dock light pedestals
18. Woodshop - Plan and repair leaks in the side walls
19. Pool area - Plan and prepare for shade cloth covering over seating area. We planned and bought the wood. Project canceled by JE
20. Parking area - Build sugar mill windows, stressed and stained.
21. Dock - Haul out and repair broken parts of the floating dock.
22. Road drainage -Install steel grates over road
23. Flagpole - Install new flagpole overlooking Dog Island
24. Helipad - Finalize design, prepare and cast new landing at heli pad, tile and grout.
25. Pool area - Remove light poles and relocate at  a/c unit.
26. Pool area - close gap between walls
Pool area - Repair the existing tiles
27. Pool area - Paint in pattern approved by JE (Ongoing)
28. Dock carpet - Install new carpet on dock
29. General maintenance - For example: Main kitchen cupboard doors, sink repairs shutters broken, Main office floor lifting
30. Staff cottage - Repair the a/c ducting that hides the pipes.
Laundry - paint the a/c unit newly installed.
 
Trust that this helps
 
Miles
